CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF DEC-30-2011 CONTINUED TO FEB-17-2012 AT 2:00 PM IN DEPARTMENT 610 TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, DEFENDANT(S) AS TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED AS TO BOTH CLAIMS WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THE ELEMENTS OF A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AGAINST THE COUNCIL NOR HAS HE ALLEGED HOW THE COUNCIL FAILED TO EXERCISE DUE CARE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS LIABILITY BASED ON THE PRINCIPLE OF RESPONDEAT SUPERIOR. PARAGRAPH 30(1) OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT OFFICE POLITICS PARTLY MOTIVATED THE ATTACK. PARAGRAPHS 13-15 AND 30(1) SUPPORT LIABILITY BASED ON A RATIFICATION THEORY. THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PARAGRAPHS 33(3) - 33(6) SUPPORT THE CLAIM THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXERCISE REASONABLE CARE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT A. BRAGG OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BRAGG & KULUVA, TEL. NO. 273-6500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Defendant has met its burden to show that there are no triable issues of fact as to claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligence. Defendant's conduct was not extreme and outrageous. Mr. Ahsan's actions towards Plaintiff were not foreseeable. In opposition, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to show that there are triable issues of fact. To the extent that Plaintiff requests a denial, Plaintiff has not sufficiently demonstrated that Defendant engaged in abuse of discovery. (CCP 437c(h); see Bothwell Decl., 6.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/LN

Off Calendar Jun-01-2012 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anthony P.X. Bothwell, Esq., of the Law Offices of Anthony Bothwell, Tel. 415.370.9571, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.

No other appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The complaint sufficiently pleads liability based on the principle of respondeat superior. Paragraph 30(1) of the Second Amended Complaint alleges that office politics partly motivated the attack. Paragraphs 13-15 and 30(1) support liability based on a ratification theory. The Court overrules the demurrer to the negligence cause of action. Paragraphs 33(3) - 33(6) support the claim that plaintiff failed to exercise reasonable care. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan Gille, Esq., of Bragg & Kuluva, appearing for Defendant and moving party.

No other appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend. The Court previously struck those allegations per the November 15, 2011 order and the allegations add nothing to the complaint given that plaintiff is not seeking punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: James J. McBride; Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918
